carlos_fixes_verification.honorific_night_owl_daytime_bug: VERIFIED FIXED — isAfterHours guard correctly prevents night owl during business hours. Screenshot shows night owl at 21:19 which is appropriate.
carlos_fixes_verification.paused_pill_touch_text: PARTIALLY FIXED — Text correctly says Tap to refresh on touch devices. BUT the pill has pointer-events:none in CSS AND onClick={onResume} references an undefined variable in App scope. Functionally works via window-level touchstart listener, but the code is broken in two simultaneous ways.
carlos_fixes_verification.ghost_tabIndex_a11y: VERIFIED FIXED — Ghost characters get tabIndex={-1} because handleClick is undefined for them.
carlos_fixes_verification.daytime_sky_brightness: VERIFIED FIXED — .office-window__sky[data-daytime] CSS rule present. Hour ranges 0-4/5-8/9-16/17-19/20-23 now fully covered.
carlos_fixes_verification.orphaned_team_vibe_mobile: VERIFIED FIXED — Dashboard screenshot shows all 5 stat cards rendering cleanly.

suggestions.title: onResume is undefined in App component header — dead click handler regression
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1151: onClick={onResume} references a variable that does not exist in App scope. onResume is only defined as an inline prop passed to StatsBar (line 1216). Worse, the CSS for .polling-paused-pill sets pointer-events:none, so the click never fires and the undefined reference is silently masked. The pill text says Tap to refresh which works ONLY because window-level touchstart/mousemove listeners exist. The pill click itself is a no-op with a broken handler hiding behind pointer-events. Fix: define a handleResume callback in App and use it in both StatsBar and the header pill, and remove pointer-events:none so the pill is actually clickable as intended.

suggestions.title: Paused pill says Tap to refresh but CSS pointer-events:none blocks the tap
suggestions.description: The .polling-paused-pill has pointer-events:none; user-select:none in App.css making it explicitly non-interactive. But it tells mobile users Tap to refresh. A user tapping the pill directly gets nothing — the tap passes through to whatever is underneath. The resume only works incidentally because any touchstart on the window triggers the listener. This is a semantic mismatch and a UX lie. Either remove pointer-events:none and wire up a real click handler, or change the copy to Touch anywhere to refresh to match actual behavior.

suggestions.title: Activity filter buttons use role=radio but lack arrow-key navigation
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1311-1340: filter bars use role=radio on button elements inside role=radiogroup. A proper radio group requires arrow-key cycling between options (not just tab/click). Screen reader users will hear radio button but arrow keys do not navigate between filters. Either use actual input type=radio with styled labels, implement arrow-key handlers between filter buttons, or switch to role=button with aria-pressed which accurately describes toggle behavior.

suggestions.title: Carlos idle card renders orphaned CURRENT TASK label with no task text
suggestions.description: Dashboard screenshot shows Carlos card with the bold CURRENT TASK header and nothing beneath it — worker.task is empty. The worker-task div renders the header unconditionally then outputs worker.task which is falsy. Results in a floating label with dead space. Guard the task section or show a No active task placeholder. The 100% progress bar on an IDLE worker with no task is also a confusing state.

suggestions.title: Bloberto speech bubble clips outside office-floor container at top
suggestions.description: Desktop screenshot clearly shows the speech bubble This could have been a commit message. rendering above the office-floor boundary. The bubble is absolutely positioned relative to Character near the top of the floor. Fix: add overflow:visible to the container, or implement bubble direction flipping (show below when near top edge). Right now it looks like Bloberto is dreaming outside the building.

suggestions.title: Completion toast and plant toast use duplicated inline style objects — third time asking
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1098-1121 and Office.jsx lines 1218-1241 both duplicate 12+ lines of identical inline styles: position:fixed, border-radius, box-shadow, animation, z-index, padding. Extract a .toast-popup CSS class and use style only for the dynamic border-color. This has been flagged in at least 3 previous reviews. At this point it is a tradition.

suggestions.title: Mobile office floor still illegible — sub-readability text and cramped layout
suggestions.description: At 375px the office floor compresses desk text to ~0.3-0.4rem. Nameplates, worker names, weather labels, and conference table text become unreadable. The office view on mobile is purely decorative. Consider: hiding sub-pixel text on mobile, making the floor horizontally scrollable with fixed min-width, or introducing a simplified character-grid view for narrow viewports.

suggestions.title: Tab button text truncated on narrow mobile viewport — Office becomes Offic
suggestions.description: At 375px the Office tab label truncates to the emoji plus four letters. The tab-toggle container does not give enough room. Quick fix: use shorter labels on small screens via CSS media query, or constrain tab text to just the emoji on mobile.

suggestions.title: Worker filter in activity log uses allWorkers fallback instead of canonical roster
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1330: worker filter list uses roster.length > 0 ? roster : allWorkers. When roster is empty only currently active workers appear as filter options. Should always use the full known roster so filters are stable regardless of who is currently active.

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers memo uses tick counter as time-passage proxy — confusing pattern
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 440: stuckWorkers useMemo depends on [workers, tick] where tick is a counter from useTimeTick. Date.now() is called inside and tick just forces re-evaluation. This works but is misleading — the memo claims to depend on a counter when it cares about elapsed wall time. A cleaner approach: extract nowMs at render time and include it, or skip the memo since the calculation is cheap.

suggestions.title: Footer tagline pagination counter breaks the vibe
suggestions.description: The footer shows 1/9 inline after the rotating tagline. At low opacity it is subtle but it turns a whimsical quote into a paginator. Sprint velocity is just vibes with a spreadsheet... 1/9. The magic evaporates. Remove the counter or show it only on hover as a tooltip.

summary: Review 50 and the scoreboard says 3.5, down from 3.9. Carlos verified fixes land mostly clean: TEAM VIBE card is back where it belongs, ghost tabIndex fixed, daytime sky brightness wired up, honorific logic is time-gated correctly. Four out of five, not bad. But the paused pill fix is a two-bug pile: pointer-events:none in CSS AND onClick referencing an undefined onResume variable in App scope. The pill says Tap to refresh and tapping it does nothing — they cancel each other out and call it shipped. The carry-overs watch from the corner for the fourth straight review: inline toast styles, radio a11y, mobile cramping, speech bubble clipping. The code is charming and the product has real personality. The execution needs tighter QA before declaring a fix done.
